title: I Am Buying Apartment REITs Hand Over Fist
text: Major private equity groups are investing in the REIT sector. They are buying apartment REITs at 60-70 cents on the dollar. So am I and here's why.

title: Buried Treasures: 10 Dramatically Undervalued REITs Yielding Over 5%
text: REITs have experienced a significant drop in value due to rising interest rates and inflation. However, with inflation under control and interest rates expected to stabilize or decrease, REITs are poised for a surge. A list of 28 undervalued REITs with a yield of 5% or better is provided, along with 10 additional REITs to consider.

title: Better REIT Buy: Camden Property Trust Or Mid-America Apartment Communities
text: CPT and MAA are two popular apartment REITs. They share many similarities, but there are also some differences. We explain which we prefer and why.

title: Fed Says 'Not So Fast'
text: Following the best week of the year, U.S. equity markets were mixed this week as interest rates rebounded from the prior week's plunge after Fed officials pushed back on market optimism. Posting gains in nine out of ten sessions since dipping into "correction territory" at the end of October, the S&P 500 gained 1.4% on the week, but Mid-Caps and Small-Caps posted declines. Following their best week since 2020, REITs took a step back this week as upward pressure on benchmark interest rates offset a solid conclusion to a strong earnings season.

title: Billionaire Investor Says 'Buy REITs': Our Top Picks
text: Barry Sternlicht is very bullish on REITs. Valuations are the lowest in years, and he is buying. We present three of our Top Picks to profit.

title: New REIT Acquisition Target
text: REITs are increasingly buying back their own shares as the best real estate investment. Share buybacks are the focus of the third quarter earnings season for REITs. Factors such as strong financial conditions, weak property acquisition spreads, and high implied cap rates make buybacks attractive.

title: Camden Property Trust Closes $500 Million 5.850% Senior Unsecured Note Offering
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) today announced it has closed the offering of $500 million senior unsecured notes due 2026. These three-year notes were issued at 99.997% of par value with a coupon of 5.850%. After giving effect to deducting the underwriting discounts and other expenses of the offering, the effective interest rate on the notes is 6.08% per annum. In connection with the offering of the notes, Camden initiated a forward interest rate swap agreement with.

title: 3 Dividend Monsters I'm Putting $15,000 In To Right Now
text: The Dividend Aristocrats index is down 5.5% YTD, compared to a 10.5% gain for the S&P 500.

title: Major landlords, RealPage sued in DC for alleged rent fixing scheme
text: D.C. Attorney General Brian Schwalb announced a lawsuit against 14 landlords in the district and RealPage for allegedly illegally raising rent prices.

title: Camden Property Trust: A Tad Too High
text: Most analysts are optimistic about Camden Property Trust's prospects, but we take a different view. Slower rental growth and a cautious outlook by the REIT's management suggest trouble lies ahead. The REIT's heavy variable-rate debt load, substantial labor liability, and valuation issues contribute to a bearish thesis.
